statement.markdown: Given an array of integers, implement a function to sort it using the Quick Sort algorithm. The provided code attempts to sort the array but fails to do so due to an incorrect swap operation. Identify and fix the bug to make the code work correctly.
statement.inputFormat: The input will be an array of integers.
statement.outputFormat: The output should be the sorted array of integers.
statement.notes: This is a hard difficulty task as it requires in-depth understanding of the Quick Sort algorithm and its implementation.

hints.text: First, understand how the Quick Sort algorithm works, then identify the incorrect swap operation in the provided code.

editorial.title: Quick Sort Algorithm
editorial.intuition: The Quick Sort algorithm works by selecting a pivot element, partitioning the array around it, and recursively sorting the subarrays.
editorial.bruteForce: A brute-force approach to solving this problem would involve implementing Quick Sort from scratch, which is inefficient and not recommended.
editorial.betterSolution: Identifying and fixing the bug in the provided code is a more efficient approach.
editorial.optimalSolution: The optimal solution involves implementing Quick Sort with an efficient partitioning scheme, such as the Lomuto partition scheme.
editorial.proofOfCorrectness: The correctness of the solution can be proven by analyzing the time and space complexity, as well as the logic behind the implementation.
editorial.dryRun: A dry run can be performed to identify any logical errors or issues with the implementation.
